verification_method: Execution, inspection, fuzzing, and benchmark evidence.
expected_result:
- The parser distinguishes long and short headers correctly.
- Truncated inputs fail consistently.
- Shared wire-shape tests are attached to the RFC 8999 requirements they prove.
- The quality lane surfaces the evidence in the repo-local reports without missing downstream trace or verification links.
procedure: Run the RFC 8999 and overlap-focused unit and property tests.
Run the header and version-negotiation fuzz suites.
Capture the long-header parsing benchmark output.
Run the repo-local quality evidence scripts.
Re-run the JSON render and validation checks so the downstream trace surface reflects the updated requirement links.
scope:
Verify the RFC 8999 long-header invariant slice, including the shared wire
shape used by version negotiation, the connection-ID length rules, and the
preservation of opaque version-specific data.
status_summary: Passed on 2026-04-30. The focused RFC 8999 requirement-home filter passed 22/22, the fuzz project built successfully, SharpFuzz instrumented the built `Incursa.Quic.dll` dependency, stdin smoke through the fuzz executable passed, and the `QuicHeaderParsingBenchmarks` Dry job executed 9/9 benchmarks. BenchmarkDotNet reported expected Dry minimum-iteration-time warnings, so this is execution evidence only and not a performance claim.
title: QUIC RFC 8999 Invariants Verification
preconditions: The RFC 8999 requirement traits are discoverable by the quality inventory.
The parser, property, fuzz, and benchmark suites remain runnable in the local workbench flow.
